oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Choose with Caution: The Historical and Contemporary Nature of the American School Choice Movement
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Schul, James
Mayer, Shauna
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This essay examines the school choice movement in the United States by examining its historical roots as well as its nature as a contemporary policy practice. We share five areas where the public should heed caution when considering school choice as a policy approach: 1) its tendency to further segregate schools by race and social class; 2) its excessive entanglement between religion and the state; 3) its threat to the teaching profession; 4) its inadequate support for public education; and 5) the incongruence between its rhetoric and evidence. Further discussion and policy alternatives are provided.
oai_dc:dc.dc:publisher: Society for the Study of Curriculum History
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: Curriculum History; Vol. 2026 No. 1 (2026): Curriculum History 2026
